hero.title: Know your car's vitals
hero.subtitle: Track maintenance, spot what's wrong, and get answers from an AI mechanic who knows your car.

symptomChecker.title: Symptom checker
symptomChecker.description: Squealing brakes? Suspension knock? Find the likely cause.

serviceSchedule.title: Service schedule
serviceSchedule.description: Oil, filters, fluids — know what's due and when.

aiChat.title: AI mechanic chat
aiChat.description: Ask anything, get answers tuned to your exact car.

tutorials.title: Tutorials
tutorials.description: Step-by-step guides with photos and videos for common fixes.

serviceSchedulePage.title: Service schedule
serviceSchedulePage.subtitle: Check when it's time to replace the part in question
serviceSchedulePage.disclaimer: Values shown are general industry averages and may not reflect your specific vehicle. Always check your vehicle's manufacturer service manual for exact intervals.

symptomChecker.title: Symptom checker
symptomChecker.subtitle: Pick the part of your car, then describe what's happening.

symptoms.softPedal: Brake pedal feels soft or spongy
symptoms.pull: Car pulls to one side while driving
symptoms.bounce: Excessive bouncing after hitting a bump
symptoms.warningLight: Check engine or battery light on

causes.wornBrakePads: Worn brake pads (wear indicator touching rotor)
causes.glazedBrakePads: Glazed brake pads or rotor surface
causes.dustDebrisPadRotor: Dust or debris between pad and rotor
causes.padsWornToMetal: Brake pads completely worn down to metal backing
causes.debrisInCaliper: Stone or debris caught in brake caliper
causes.brakeFluidLeak: Brake fluid leak at caliper or wheel cylinder
causes.unevenPadDeposits: Uneven brake pad deposits on rotor surface
causes.swayBarEndLinks: Worn or broken sway bar end links
causes.shockAbsorberMounts: Loose or worn shock absorber mounts
causes.unevenTirePressure: Uneven tire pressure between left and right
causes.seizedCaliper: Seized brake caliper dragging on one side
causes.wornStrut: Worn or collapsed strut on one side
causes.wornShockAbsorbers: Worn or blown shock absorbers / struts
causes.wornCvJoint: Worn CV joint or CV axle boot
causes.wornTieRodEnds: Loose or worn tie rod ends
causes.wornSerpentineBelt: Worn or loose serpentine / accessory belt
causes.failingIdlerPulley: Failing idler pulley or tensioner bearing
causes.glazedBeltSurface: Glazed belt surface from oil contamination
causes.lowOilLevel: Low engine oil level or pressure
causes.wornValveLifters: Worn or stuck valve lifters / tappets
causes.failingIdleAirControl: Dirty or failing idle air control valve / throttle body
causes.failingFuelPump: Failing fuel pump unable to maintain pressure
causes.badGroundConnection: Bad ground connection in lighting circuit
causes.failedFuelPump: Failed fuel pump not delivering fuel
causes.brokenTimingBeltChain: Broken timing belt / chain (engine won't fire)
causes.parasiticDraw: Parasitic draw from a relay or module staying active
causes.oldFailingBattery: Old or failing battery that no longer holds charge
causes.faultyAlternatorNotCharging: Faulty alternator not charging while driving
causes.looseFuelCap: Loose or missing fuel filler cap (EVAP leak)
causes.weakBatteryVoltageFault: Weak battery causing voltage fault codes
